mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate at near-zero levels (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) signals maximum monetary accommodation. Under this regime, equity risk premiums compress and discount rates fall, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities seeking yield.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window in zero-rate regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration and recessionary pressure. Equity investors interpret rising joblessness as a forward indicator of demand destruction, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as sentiment shifts.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 135489k as of 2008-12-15, down from prior months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite payroll declines, or correlation between PAYEMS momentum and 5-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Joint deterioration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals broad-based demand collapse across consumer and residential sectors. This dual weakness triggers equity selloffs as investors recognize earnings downgrades are imminent, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 1% month-over-month AND HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 343242M and HOUST at 625k as of 2008-11-15
regime_hint: synchronized demand shock across consumer and housing
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when both RSAFS and HOUST decline simultaneously, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines signal supply-side weakness and capacity utilization collapse. Equity investors interpret falling production as evidence of demand destruction and margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings guidance deteriorates.
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns are not significantly lower following INDPRO declines > 1.5% (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 7% signals labor market stress and consumer income erosion. Higher unemployment reduces household purchasing power and confidence, triggering equity selloffs as investors anticipate lower consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: elevated unemployment and labor market weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE > 7%, or correlation between UNRATE level and 20-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) declining month-over-month signals deflationary pressure and demand weakness. Deflation increases real debt burdens and reduces nominal growth expectations, causing equity investors to lower earnings forecasts and compress valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns are not significantly lower following CPIAUCSL declines > 0.3% (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) both accelerating month-over-month signal demand recovery and consumer confidence stabilization. This dual demand strength triggers equity rallies as investors anticipate earnings stabilization and multiple expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises > 3% month-over-month AND RSAFS rises > 0.5% month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances when both HOUST and RSAFS accelerate, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) both rising month-over-month signal synchronized supply and labor expansion. This dual strength indicates robust economic activity and earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors increase equity allocations.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 0.5% month-over-month AND PAYEMS rises > 0.2% month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when both INDPRO and PAYEMS expand, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).
